authors: Sebastián Valenzuela Ingrid Bachmann Porismita Borah Natalia Solís Valdés
Chilec Millennium Nucleus on Digital Inequalities and Opportunities (NUDOS)
raw_title: The Effects of Generative AI in News on Media Credibility and Selectivity: Evidence from a Conjoint Experiment in Chile
title_en: The Effects of Generative AI in News on Media Credibility and Selectivity: Evidence from a Conjoint Experiment in Chile
title_zh: The Effects of Generative AI in News on Media Credibility and Selectivity: Evidence from a Conjoint Experiment in Chile
plain_summary_en: This paper studies The Effects of Generative AI in News on Media Credibility and Selectivity: Evidence from a Conjoint Experiment in Chile. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Credibility, Generative grammar, Conjoint analysis, Generative model. The abstract suggests a experiment approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
plain_summary_zh: This paper studies The Effects of Generative AI in News on Media Credibility and Selectivity: Evidence from a Conjoint Experiment in Chile. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Credibility, Generative grammar, Conjoint analysis, Generative model. The abstract suggests a experiment approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Credibility, Generative grammar, Conjoint analysis, Generative model, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
research_direction_zh: The paper points to an active research direction around Credibility, Generative grammar, Conjoint analysis, Generative model, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
